───────────────────────────────────────
   All 7 program addresses are INVALID Solana format:
   
   ❌ Votejju1omngnwg           (16 chars, invalid format)
   ❌ NFTfhr4ph5w0ug            (16 chars, invalid format)
   ❌ Botzcv45lq3rar            (15 chars, contains invalid '0')
   ❌ Botgy74odomne             (13 chars, contains invalid '0')
   ❌ Botfpocp7jkxr             (13 chars, invalid format)
   ❌ Botbsm7et3k5r             (13 chars, contains invalid '0')
   ❌ Botnk1s2xskl7             (13 chars, contains invalid '0')
   
   Expected Format: 44-character base58 string
   Example: 11111111111111111111111111111111 (44 chars)


Finding #2: AUTHORITY ADDRESS INVALID
──────────────────────────────────────
   Master Authority: zhBqbd9tSQFPevg4188JxcgpccCj3t1Jxb29zsBc2R4
   
   Status: ❌ INVALID
   └─ Contains invalid base58 characters
   └─ Not a valid Solana address format
   └─ Cannot query on-chain

🤔 ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Hypothesis #1: Placeholder/Encoded Addresses (⭐ MOST LIKELY)
─────────────────────────────────────────────
  • Addresses appear to be demo/placeholder format
  • Readable strings (Vote, NFT, Bot prefixes)
  • Intentionally shortened for documentation
  • NOT actual Solana program IDs
  
  Probability: 🔴 HIGH (85%)
  Action: Find real program IDs from deployment logs


Hypothesis #2: Programs Not Yet Deployed (⭐ LIKELY)
──────────────────────────────────────────
  • Addresses might be invalid even if formatted correctly
  • Programs may not be deployed to mainnet
  • No RPC response indicates no on-chain account
  
  Probability: 🟠 MEDIUM (10%)
  Action: Check if programs need to be deployed


Hypothesis #3: Programs on Different Network (UNLIKELY)
───────────────────────────────────────────
  • Programs might be on devnet instead of mainnet
  • Different deployment target used
  
  Probability: 🔵 LOW (5%)
  Action: Query devnet RPC instead


🔧 WHAT THIS MEANS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

The deployment data contains PLACEHOLDER ADDRESSES that are:
├─ NOT valid Solana addresses
├─ NOT queryable on blockchain
├─ NOT representing actual on-chain programs
└─ Likely for DOCUMENTATION/DEMO purposes only


🎯 NEXT ACTIONS (Priority Order)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

1. [CRITICAL] Find Real Program IDs
   ├─ Search deployment logs
   ├─ Check GitHub Actions workflow outputs
   ├─ Look in .cache/ for deployment receipts
   ├─ Search for transaction hashes
   └─ Time: 30 minutes


2. [HIGH] Verify Real Programs On-Chain
   ├─ Once you have real program IDs (44-char base58)
   ├─ Query Solana mainnet RPC
   ├─ Check Solana Explorer
   ├─ Verify program owner
   └─ Time: 15 minutes


3. [HIGH] Update Documentation
   ├─ Replace placeholder addresses with real IDs
   ├─ Update program-inventory.json
   ├─ Update deployment records
   └─ Time: 1 hour


4. [MEDIUM] Setup Monitoring
   ├─ Monitor real program IDs for changes
   ├─ Alert on ownership transfer
   ├─ Track program upgrades
   └─ Time: 2-3 hours


📁 FILES TO CHECK FOR REAL PROGRAM IDS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

1. .cache/final-deployment.json
   └─ Treasury deployment (may have real mints)

2. Deployment logs in .cache/
   └─ *-deployment*.json files
   └─ *-deploy*.log files

3. GitHub Actions workflow outputs
   └─ .github/workflows/deploy*.yml execution logs
   └─ GitHub Actions run history

4. Blockchain history
   └─ Check recent transactions on Solana Explorer
   └─ Filter by authority address

🚀 RECOMMENDED SOLUTION
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

OPTION A: Deploy to Mainnet
────────────────────────────
  If programs haven't been deployed yet:
  1. Use Anchor/Solana CLI to deploy
  2. Capture real program IDs from deployment output
  3. Update all records with real IDs
  4. Verify on Solana Explorer
  Time: 2-4 hours


OPTION B: Locate Existing Deployment
─────────────────────────────────────
  If programs are already deployed:
  1. Search GitHub Actions logs for real IDs
  2. Check blockchain explorer for transactions
  3. Find deployment transaction hashes
  4. Extract real program IDs
  Time: 1-2 hours


OPTION C: Deploy to Devnet (Testing)
──────────────────────────────────────
  If you want to verify deployments:
  1. Deploy to Solana devnet
  2. Test program functionality
  3. Verify with devnet RPC
  4. Then deploy to mainnet with real IDs
  Time: 1-3 hours


💡 KEY INSIGHTS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

1. Addresses are Placeholder Format
   └─ Likely intentional for documentation/demo

2. Authority Address Also Invalid
   └─ Complete set of placeholder data

3. No Actual Blockchain Verification Possible
   └─ Need real Solana addresses

4. Deployment Data May Be Incomplete
   └─ Real IDs stored elsewhere OR
   └─ Programs not yet deployed

5. All 7 Programs Share Same Issue
   └─ Consistent with placeholder pattern


⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

• These placeholder addresses are NOT operational
• Programs cannot be interacted with using these IDs
• Actual program functionality requires real addresses
• All balance/ownership queries will fail
• On-chain verification impossible until real IDs found

The deployment appears to be a TEMPLATE or DEMO with:
  ✓ Correct structure and fields
  ✓ Placeholder addresses for documentation
  ✓ Realistic-looking program names
  ✗ NOT ready for production blockchain interaction
